auth.forgotPasswordDesc: Enter your email and we'll send you a reset link.
auth.twoFactorDesc: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
auth.forgotPasswordSuccess: If an account exists with that email address, we've sent a password reset link. Please check your inbox.
auth.passwordMinError: Password must be at least 8 characters.
auth.passwordResetRedirecting: Password reset successfully! Redirecting to login...
auth.invalidResetToken: Invalid or missing reset token. Please request a new password reset link.
auth.enterBackupCode: Enter one of your backup codes
auth.enter6DigitCode: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app
auth.emailVerifiedDesc: Your email has been successfully verified. You can now sign in.
auth.failedToComplete: Failed to complete authentication. Please try again.

dashboard.noServicesYet: No services yet. Deploy your first service to get started.

services.confirmDestroy: Are you sure you want to destroy this service? This action cannot be undone.

domains.title: Domains
domains.noDomainsDesc: Add a domain to get started.
domains.confirmRemove: Are you sure you want to remove this domain?
domains.customNsWarning: Using custom nameservers means DNS records managed in Fleet won't resolve for this domain.

domainPicker.title: Domain Configuration
domainPicker.description: Optionally assign a domain to route traffic to your service.
domainPicker.placeholder: Select a domain or type a custom one
domainPicker.noDomainsHint: Register a domain in the Domains page first, or type a custom one.

volumeSharing.allServicesShare: All services will share a single storage volume

storagePage.name: Name
storagePage.nameHint: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.
storagePage.confirmDelete: Delete this volume? All data will be lost.
storagePage.shrinkWarning: Shrinking a volume cannot be undone. Make sure you have backups.

storageSetup.title: Storage
storageSetup.replicationDesc: Your data is replicated across {factor} nodes. Up to {failCount} node(s) can fail without data loss.
storageSetup.noStorageDesc: Set up your storage system to enable volume replication, object storage for uploads and backups, and automatic failover across nodes.
storageSetup.chooseModeDesc: Select how you want Fleet to manage storage across your infrastructure.
storageSetup.localDesc: For development or single-server setups. No replication. Uses local filesystem and NFS. This is the current default.
storageSetup.distributedDesc: Production-grade. Replicates data across 3+ nodes with automatic failover and self-healing.
storageSetup.glusterfsDesc: Distributed replicated filesystem. Great for bare metal and VMs. POSIX-compatible, auto-heal, Docker volume plugin.
storageSetup.cephDesc: Enterprise-grade block storage. Scales to exabytes. Erasure coding, snapshots, thin provisioning. Industry standard.
storageSetup.minioDesc: Self-hosted S3-compatible. Runs on your own nodes. No vendor lock-in.
storageSetup.s3Desc: Amazon S3 or S3-compatible services (Backblaze B2, Wasabi, etc.)
storageSetup.gcsDesc: Google Cloud Storage. Native GCS integration for GCP deployments.
storageSetup.addNodesDesc: Add at least {count} nodes to form the storage cluster. Each node stores a full copy of your data.
storageSetup.localNoNodes: Local mode uses the current server. No additional nodes needed.
storageSetup.localModeInfo: Local mode uses this server's filesystem. All volumes and objects are stored on a single node. You can upgrade to distributed mode later.
storageSetup.nodeSetupGuideDesc: Prerequisites, firewall ports, security, and step-by-step instructions
storageSetup.overviewPart1: Each storage node is a server (bare metal or VM) that stores and replicates your data. Nodes can be
storageSetup.overviewMinNodes: You need at least {count} nodes for {count}-way replication.
storageSetup.staticIpDesc: Each storage node must have a fixed IP address (static assignment or DHCP reservation). Changing IPs will break the storage cluster.
storageSetup.privateNetworkDesc: Storage traffic should stay on a private/internal network (e.g. 10.0.x.x). Do not expose storage ports to the public internet.
storageSetup.lowLatencyDesc: All storage nodes should be in the same datacenter or availability zone. High latency will degrade performance and replication speed.
storageSetup.storageOnlyNoSwarm: Storage-only nodes do NOT need Docker Swarm.
storageSetup.storageOnlyNoSwarmDesc: Dedicated storage nodes run only the storage services ({services}). They are managed by Fleet over SSH.
storageSetup.storageComputeSwarm: Storage+Compute nodes are part of Docker Swarm.
storageSetup.storageComputeSwarmDesc: These nodes join the swarm as workers AND run storage. Use this when you want fewer servers doing both jobs.
storageSetup.swarmNeedsClient: Swarm worker nodes need the volume client.
storageSetup.swarmNeedsClientDesc: Any Docker Swarm node that runs containers with storage volumes needs the {client} installed so it can mount volumes.
storageSetup.firewallPortsDesc: Open these ports between storage nodes and from Swarm workers to storage nodes. Only allow traffic from your internal network.
storageSetup.sshServiceDesc: SSH — Fleet manages nodes via SSH
storageSetup.neverExposePorts: Never expose storage ports ({ports}) to the public internet. Use ufw or iptables to restrict access to your internal network CIDR only.
storageSetup.secSshKeyPart2: (disable password login). Fleet connects to nodes via SSH for management operations.
storageSetup.secVlanPart2: separate from public-facing services if possible.
storageSetup.secGlusterfsPart2: . Ensure only trusted IPs can reach port 24007. Consider TLS encryption for GlusterFS (available in v3.12+).
storageSetup.secCephPart2: by default. Keep the keyring file secure and restrict access to the admin user.
storageSetup.secMinioPart2: for production. Use strong access key and secret key (auto-generated during setup).
storageSetup.secUpdatesPart1: Keep the operating system and storage software
storageSetup.secUpdatesPart2: with security patches. Enable unattended-upgrades for automatic security updates.
storageSetup.quickSetup: Quick Setup — Run on Each Storage Node
storageSetup.quickSetupDesc: SSH into each node and run the following commands to prepare it for Fleet storage.
storageSetup.swarmWorkersInstall: On Docker Swarm workers (so containers can mount volumes):
storageSetup.configureReplicationDesc: Set the replication factor and object storage settings.
storageSetup.threeWayDesc: Survives 2 node failures. Recommended for production.
storageSetup.customEndpointHint: for S3-compatible services like Backblaze B2, Wasabi
storageSetup.gcsWorkloadIdentityNote: If running on GCP with workload identity, you can leave the key file empty. The GCS client will use the default credentials automatically.
storageSetup.localNoConfig: Local mode requires no additional configuration. Storage will use the local filesystem on this server.
storageSetup.initializeDesc: This will set up the storage system and verify everything is working.
storageSetup.clickInitDistributed: Click the button below to initialize the GlusterFS cluster and MinIO.
storageSetup.clickInitLocal: Click the button below to initialize the local storage system.
storageSetup.migrateExistingDesc: Move your existing volumes, uploads, and backups to the new storage system.
storageSetup.noDataToMigrate: No existing data to migrate. You can skip this step.
storageSetup.migrationOnlineNotice: Services will continue running during migration. A brief switchover happens at the end when the active provider is changed.
storageSetup.readyDistributed: Your storage system is ready. All volumes and objects will now use the distributed cluster.
storageSetup.readyLocal: Your storage system is ready. All volumes and objects will now use the local filesystem.
storageSetup.migrateStorageProviderDesc: Move all data between storage providers with zero downtime.
storageSetup.servicesOnline: Services will remain online during migration
storageSetup.briefSwitchover: A brief switchover occurs when data is fully copied
storageSetup.pauseResume: You can pause and resume the migration at any time
storageSetup.rollbackAvailable: Rollback is available if anything goes wrong
storageSetup.migrationCompleteDesc: All data has been migrated to the new providers. The storage system is now using {volumeProvider} for volumes and {objectProvider} for objects.
storageSetup.readyAutoConfigPending: Ready — {auto} optional port(s) will be configured during setup
storageSetup.requiredNotRunning: Required — install this service before proceeding
storageSetup.migrationFailedCheckLog: Migration failed. Check the log for details.
storageSetup.attachNodeDesc: Add a new storage node to the existing cluster.
storageSetup.detachNodeDesc: Remove an individual node from the cluster. If using GlusterFS, the peer will be detached first.
storageSetup.detachConfirmDesc: This will remove the node from the storage cluster. Existing data on remaining nodes will not be affected, but replication factor may be reduced.
storageSetup.resetAllDesc: Remove all storage nodes and reset the cluster configuration. This does not delete stored data on the nodes themselves.
storageSetup.resetConfirmTitle: Are you sure you want to reset all storage?
storageSetup.resetConfirmDesc: This will remove all storage nodes from the database and delete the cluster configuration. You will need to reconfigure storage from scratch. Enter your password to confirm.

backups.title: Backups
backups.noBackupsDesc: Create a backup to protect your data.
backups.confirmRestore: Are you sure you want to restore this backup? Current data will be overwritten.
backups.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this backup?

ssh.title: SSH Keys
ssh.noKeysDesc: Add an SSH key for secure access.

ssh.confirmRemove: Are you sure you want to remove this SSH key?

apiKeys.noKeysDesc: Create an API key for programmatic access.
apiKeys.name: Key Name
apiKeys.confirmRevoke: Are you sure you want to revoke this API key?
apiKeys.copyWarning: Make sure to copy your API key now. You won't be able to see it again!

marketplace.title: App Marketplace
marketplace.noEnvVars: No environment variables. Click + Add to configure.
marketplace.resourceLimitDisclaimer: Resource limits per container: max {cpu} millicores CPU, {memory} MB memory, {replicas} replicas. Values exceeding account limits will be capped.
marketplace.estimatedCost: Estimated monthly cost: {cost}/mo (based on current pricing rates)
marketplace.composeEditorHint: Advanced: Edit the raw YAML template. Changes here override the template defaults.
marketplace.deployPartial: {name} deployed, but {count} service(s) failed to start. Check the services page for details.
marketplace.noPricing: Pricing not configured. Contact your administrator to set up billing rates.

deploy.title: Deploy
deploy.dockerImageDesc: Deploy from a Docker Hub or private registry image.
deploy.fromGithubDesc: Deploy from a GitHub repository with automatic builds.
deploy.fromMarketplaceDesc: Deploy a pre-configured app from the marketplace.
deploy.noPorts: No ports exposed. Add a port mapping to make the service accessible.

github.connectDesc: Connect your GitHub account to browse your repositories and deploy directly from your codebase with automatic builds.
github.autoDeployDesc: Automatically redeploy when new commits are pushed to the selected branch.

deploy.fromUploadDesc: Upload a project archive (zip, tar) or folder and deploy it.
deploy.dragDrop: Drop your archive here or click to browse
deploy.buildFileHint: Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml path. Leave empty to auto-detect, or skip if not needed yet.

deployGh.title: Deploy from GitHub
deployGh.noManifest: This repository does not include a fleet.json manifest. You can still deploy it, but you will need to configure environment variables and ports manually.

fileExplorer.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"?
fileExplorer.dropHere: Drop files or folders here to upload
fileExplorer.dropToUpload: Drag and drop files or folders here
fileExplorer.name: Name
fileExplorer.rebuildDesc: Upload a new archive to replace source files and trigger a rebuild.

service.githubBranchHint: The branch to watch for pushes and PR merges.
service.autoDeployDesc: Automatically deploy when code is pushed or a PR is merged into this branch.

billing.title: Billing
billing.removeMethodConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this payment method?
billing.setDefaultFailed: Failed to set default payment method

billing.cancelledBanner: Your subscription has been cancelled. Access continues until

settings.title: Settings
settings.twoFactorDesc: Add an extra layer of security to your account.
settings.scanQrCode: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app
settings.enterCode: Enter the code from your app to confirm
settings.backupCodesDesc: Save these codes in a safe place. Each code can only be used once.
settings.deleteAccountDesc: Schedule your account for deletion. All services will be stopped immediately and the account will be permanently deleted after 30 days.
settings.confirmDelete: This action cannot be undone. All your services, domains, and data will be permanently deleted.
settings.deleteConfirmDesc: Your account will be scheduled for deletion in 30 days. All running services will be stopped immediately. You can cancel the deletion at any time during the grace period.
settings.passwordRequired: Password is required to confirm deletion
settings.deletionScheduled: Account has been scheduled for deletion
settings.deletionScheduledDesc: This account will be permanently deleted on {date} ({days} days remaining). All services have been stopped.
settings.deletionScheduledNote: You can cancel the deletion at any time before the scheduled date. After deletion, all data will be permanently removed.
settings.disconnectConfirm: Are you sure you want to disconnect this account from its parent? This cannot be undone.
settings.slugHint: This is used in URLs and API calls.
settings.disconnectParentDesc: Remove this account from its parent organization. This cannot be undone.

services.noServices: No services found across any accounts.

settings.title: Platform Settings
settings.rootDomainDesc: The base domain for your platform (e.g. fleet.example.com). Used for SFTP, database connections, webhooks, and DNS.
settings.domainRegistrarDesc: Configure domain registrar API for selling domains.
settings.domainPricingDesc: Configure prices for each TLD sold to customers.
settings.noTldPricing: No TLD pricing configured. Click "Sync from Provider" to fetch prices.
settings.githubConfigDesc: Configure GitHub OAuth for auto-deploy from repositories.
settings.githubOAuthHint: Create a GitHub OAuth App at github.com/settings/developers. Set the callback URL to your-domain.com/api/v1/auth/github/callback.
settings.googleConfigDesc: Configure Google OAuth to allow users to sign in with their Google account.
settings.googleOAuthHint: Create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID at console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials. Set the authorized redirect URI to your-domain.com/api/v1/auth/google/callback.
settings.brandingDesc: Customize your platform's logo, favicon, and name.
settings.logoHint: Recommended: PNG or SVG, max 2 MB
settings.faviconHint: Recommended: ICO or PNG 32x32, max 500 KB

billing.title: Billing Configuration
billing.billingModelDesc: Choose how your platform charges customers.
billing.fixedPriceDesc: Flat rate pricing per plan tier
billing.allowUserChoice: Allow end users to choose their billing model
billing.planTiersDesc: Define pricing plans your customers can subscribe to.
billing.noPlans: No plans created yet. Click "Add Plan" to create your first tier.
billing.usagePricingDesc: Set per-unit rates for resource consumption.
billing.locationPricingDesc: Enable location pricing to configure per-region cost multipliers.
billing.label: Label
billing.resourceLimitsDesc: Default limits inherited by all accounts. Override per account as needed.
billing.overridesDesc: Custom discounts and pricing for specific accounts.
billing.stripePaymentMethods: Apple Pay, Google Pay & Fast Checkout
billing.stripePaymentMethodsDesc: Enable express payment methods so your customers can pay faster with Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Stripe Link.
billing.stripeStep1: Go to Settings > Payment methods in your Stripe Dashboard
billing.stripeStep2: Enable Apple Pay, Google Pay, and optionally Link (Stripe's fast checkout)
billing.stripeStep3: For Apple Pay, verify your domain under Settings > Payment methods > Apple Pay by adding the Stripe domain verification file
billing.dataLifecycleDesc: Configure automatic suspension, deletion, and permanent purge of account data.
billing.autoSuspend: Automatically suspend accounts with overdue payments
billing.autoDelete: Automatically schedule deletion of suspended accounts
billing.volumeDeletion: Delete storage volumes when account is deleted
billing.purgeEnabled: Permanently remove soft-deleted data after retention period

sharedDomains.title: Shared Domains
sharedDomains.priceHint: Price in smallest currency unit (e.g. cents)
sharedDomains.empty: No shared domains configured. Add a domain to let users claim subdomains.
sharedDomains.confirmDelete: Delete this shared domain? All subdomain claims will be removed.
sharedDomains.dnsHint: For each shared domain, configure a wildcard DNS record (*.example.com) pointing to your platform IP. Traefik will automatically handle SSL certificates for each claimed subdomain.

setup.title: Fleet Setup
setup.subtitle: Set up your hosting platform in a few steps
setup.howToSetup: How would you like to set up this server?
setup.leaderDesc: Initialize a new Fleet platform. Create the first admin account, configure your domain, and start managing services.
setup.workerDesc: Join this server to an existing Fleet cluster. Install Docker, join the Swarm, and configure shared storage.
setup.dockerSwarmDesc: Fleet uses Docker Swarm to orchestrate your services.
setup.installDockerHint: Docker is not installed on this server.
setup.installDockerAutoHint: Run the Fleet install script to automatically install Docker and all dependencies:
setup.runJoinScript: Run the Fleet join script on this server. It will automatically install Docker, NFS, and all required dependencies:
setup.joinScriptNote: The join script handles everything: system dependencies, Docker installation, NFS client, and Swarm join configuration.
setup.manualDockerNote: Or if you prefer to install Docker manually:
setup.createAdminDesc: This will be the super administrator of your platform.
setup.passwordMin8: Password must be at least 8 characters
setup.platformConfigDesc: Basic platform settings. You can change these later in admin settings.
setup.domainHint: Leave empty for localhost development. Can be changed later.
setup.jwtNote: A secure JWT secret will be auto-generated for signing authentication tokens.
setup.setupCompleteDesc: Your platform is ready. You're logged in as the super administrator.
setup.ensureDocker: Ensure Docker is installed and running on this node.
setup.runCommand: Run the following command on your server to install Docker:
setup.enableDocker: Then enable and start the Docker service:
setup.dockerRunning: Once Docker is running, proceed to the next step.
setup.connectSwarm: Connect this node to the Docker Swarm cluster.
setup.joinTokenHint: Get this from your manager node with:
setup.nfsDesc: Set up shared NFS storage for persistent data.
setup.nodeCompleteDesc: Your node has been configured and is ready to join the Fleet platform. Click the button below to finish setup and start using the dashboard.

errors.notFoundDesc: The page you're looking for doesn't exist.
errors.networkErrorDesc: Could not connect to the server. Please check your connection.

impersonation.overridesReset: Account overrides reset to global defaults

envVars.importHint: Paste .env file contents. Lines starting with # are ignored.

tags.title: Tags
tags.noTags: No tags. Add tags to organize your services.

reseller.title: Reseller Program
reseller.allowSubAccountReselling: Allow resellers to enable reselling for sub-accounts
reseller.stripeNotConnected: Connect your Stripe account to receive markup payments
reseller.notEnabled: The reseller program is not currently enabled
reseller.suspended: Your reseller account has been suspended
reseller.customDomainHint: Point a CNAME record for this domain to your platform instance
reseller.programNotEnabledDesc: The reseller program is not currently enabled. Contact your platform administrator for more information.
reseller.applyDescription: As a reseller, you can create sub-accounts, set your own pricing with markup, customize your branding, and earn revenue by reselling our platform services under your own brand.
reseller.applicationMessagePlaceholder: Tell us about your business and how you plan to use the reseller program...
reseller.pendingDesc: Your application to become a reseller is pending review. You will be notified once it has been approved.
reseller.stripeConnectDesc: Connect your Stripe account to receive payouts from your sub-accounts. This is required to start earning revenue as a reseller.
reseller.discountNote: Set by platform administrator. Contact support to request changes.
reseller.noDiscount: No discount rate has been configured for your account.
reseller.markupPercentHint: Percentage added on top of your discounted cost (0-100)
reseller.markupFixedHint: Fixed amount in cents added on top of your discounted cost
reseller.brandDescriptionPlaceholder: A short description of your hosting service...
reseller.notEligibleDesc: Your account is not currently eligible to apply for the reseller program. Contact support for more information.

config.title: Reseller Configuration
config.description: Configure the global reseller program settings
config.enableDesc: Allow accounts to apply for the reseller program
config.approvalModeDesc: Auto-approve instantly activates reseller accounts. Manual requires admin review.
config.allowSubAccountDesc: Let resellers enable reselling for their sub-accounts

applications.title: Reseller Applications
applications.description: Review and manage pending reseller applications
applications.rejectDesc: Provide an optional note explaining the rejection reason

resellers.title: Active Resellers
resellers.description: Manage reseller accounts, discounts, and status

jobs.title: Jobs
jobs.confirmRemove: Are you sure you want to remove this job?
jobs.confirmClean: Remove all {status} jobs older than 24 hours?

confirmDelete.title: Delete Service
confirmDelete.message: Are you sure you want to delete
confirmDelete.messageStack: Are you sure you want to delete stack
confirmDelete.deleteVolumesHint: Permanently deletes all data stored in the volumes
confirmDelete.deleteVolumesWarning: Warning: All data in the associated volumes will be permanently deleted. This cannot be recovered.

updates.title: Platform Updates
updates.noReleases: No releases found. Click "Check for Updates" to fetch releases.
updates.backupBeforeDesc: Takes a database backup before each update for safe rollback
updates.resetDesc: Force-reset the update state to clear any stuck lock
updates.confirmUpdateDesc: Are you sure you want to update to {version}? This will perform a rolling update.
updates.confirmRollbackDesc: Are you sure you want to rollback to version {version}? This will revert all Docker services to their previous images.
updates.confirmMigrateDesc: Are you sure you want to run database migrations? This will apply any pending schema changes.
updates.confirmSeedDesc: Are you sure you want to run database seeders? This will insert default data.
updates.confirmResetDesc: Force-reset the update state? This clears any stuck lock and returns the system to idle. Only use if an update is truly stuck.
updates.backupDisabledWarning: WARNING: Pre-update backup is DISABLED in settings.
updates.restartingDesc: The platform is applying the update. This page will automatically refresh once the API is back online.
